task_completion.description: Verify that a new user named 'sec_admin' with the email 'security@example.com' and Administrator role has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: WordPress Users list showing 'sec_admin' with the role 'Administrator' and email 'security@example.com'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the original 'admin' user is deleted and their content was reassigned to 'sec_admin'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress Users list no longer shows the 'admin' user. There may be a confirmation banner showing deletion and reassignment, or the post count for 'sec_admin' reflects the reassigned content.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'DISALLOW_FILE_EDIT' is set to true in wp-config.php.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or text editor showing the contents of '/var/www/html/wordpress/wp-config.php' with the line `define( 'DISALLOW_FILE_EDIT', true );` or similar added to the file.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'UpdraftPlus WordPress Backup Plugin' is installed and activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: WordPress Plugins page showing 'UpdraftPlus - Backup/Restore' as installed and active, or the UpdraftPlus settings page being visible in the dashboard.

task_completion.description: Verify that both files and database backup schedules are set to 'Weekly' and the retention limit is set to '4'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: UpdraftPlus Settings tab showing dropdowns for 'Files backup schedule' and 'Database backup schedule' both set to 'Weekly', and the 'retain this many scheduled backups' input fields both set to '4'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual WordPress admin dashboard for user and plugin management.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard WordPress admin UI (wp-admin) being navigated in a web browser, not just API calls or text mockups.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually edited the wp-config.php file using a terminal or text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a terminal window with a text editor (like nano, vim) or a file manager modifying the actual '/var/www/html/wordpress/wp-config.php' file.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent didn't just type out success messages in a text editor to fake completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: Success messages (like 'User deleted') appear in the standard WordPress UI notification banners, not in a standalone text document or terminal echo.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected new admin username is 'sec_admin' with email 'security@example.com' and role 'administrator'. The original admin username to be deleted is 'admin'.
